v. There will be an inclusive provision of reservation for Person with 
Disabilities (PwD) who belongs to SC category for the consideration as 
per norms of existing quota of PwD. as per The Rights of Persons with 
Disabilities Act, 2016. The minimum degree of disability should be 40% in 
order to be eligible for any concessions/benefits. 
7. Duration of Fellowship 
Name of  
the Course  
Maximum 
duration  
Admissibility of JRF and SRF 
Junior Research 
Fellowship (JRF)  
Senior Research 
Fellowship (SRF) 
M. Phil  2 Years  2 Years  Nil 
Ph. D  5 Years  2 Years  Remaining 3 years 
M.Phil + Ph. D 5 Years 2 Years  Remaining 3 years 
  
Rate of fellowships and other facilities  
i. The rates of fellowship for JRF and SRF will be at par with the UGC 
Fellowships. The rates applicable w.e.f. 01.01.2019 are as follows: 
S No.  Head  Rates Applicable for  
  JRF SRF 
1. Fellowship in all streams. Rs. 31,000/ - p.m. for 
initial two years  
Rs. 35,000/ - p.m. for 
remaining tenure 
Page 5 of 7 
 
 
2.  Contingency for Humanities 
& Social Sciences  
Rs. 10,000/ - p.a. for 
initial two years  
Rs. 20,500/ - p.a. for 
remaining tenure. 
3.  Contingency for Sciences.  Rs. 12,000/ - p.a. for 
Initial two years 
Rs 25,000/ - p.a. for 
remaining tenure.  
4.  Escort/Reader assistance 
(All Subjects) for physically 
and visually handicapped 
candidates  
Rs. 2,000/- p.m. Rs. 2,000/- p.m. 
5.  House Rent   Allowance 
(HRA)* 
@ 8%, 16% and 24% to those students who 
are not provided with hostel accommodation, 
in accordance with Govt. of India norms as 
applicable in the city/location where the 
research fellows are working. 
  
Note regarding HRA: 
  
a. In case, hostel accommodation is provided to the scholars by their 
institutions, the scholar is eligible to draw only the hostel fee excluding 
mess, electricity, water charges etc.  
b. In case hostel accommodation offered by the university/institution is 
refused by the candidate, he/she will forfeit his/her claim of HRA. 
c. In case of non -availability of hostel accommodation, the scholar may 
be provided with single accommodation by the host institution. In such 
cases, the rent paid by the scholar on actual basis shall be reimbursed, 
subject to the ceiling of HRA as per Govt. of India norms. 
d. If the scholar makes his/her own arrangements of accommodation, 
he/she shall be entitled to draw HRA as per ceiling and categorization 
of cities by the Govt of India. 
e. In case t he scholar wishes to draw HRA, he/she is required to claim 
HRA on monthly basis by submitting monthly confirmation Certificate in 
the prescribed format, to his/her institution. 
ii. Other facilities such as medical facilities, leave including maternity leave will 
be governed as per the guidelines of UGC in case of their fellowship 
programme. 
8. Procedure for Applying for the Scheme (JRF)  
i. NSFDC is the nodal agency for implementing the National Fellowship 
scheme for SC students. 
ii. The selection for the award of fellowship to students will be made on the 
basis of merit of each UGC-NET-JRF or UGC-CSIR NET-JRF examination. 
Page 6 of 7 
 
iii. While the merit for award of fellowships under NFSC will be drawn up 
through the NET examination, preference will be given to such students 
who ha ve already secured admission. However, in case sufficient such 
candidates are not available, candidates who have not secured admission 
will also be selected in the order of merit in the NET examination. A 
separate merit list will be drawn up for such candidates if required. 
iv. Candidates who have already secured admission are required to join the 
fellowship within 3 months of issue of JRF award letter under the 
Scheme. The duration for joining the course will not be extended, 
candidature of such candidates, wh o do not join the fellowship within 3 
months, shall be cancelled. 
v. The qualified candidates, who have not secured admission at the time of 
application for Fellowship under the Scheme, are required to get 
admission for regular and full time M.Phil./Ph.D in a  recognized research 
institution at the first available opportunity but no later than three years 
from the date of award of fellowship. 
vi. Genuineness of the Scheduled Caste Certi ficate furnished by the awardee 
shall be verified by the concerned University/I nstitution before allowing 
the awardee to avail fellowship under the scheme. 
vii. The decision of the NSFDC in regard to awarding the Fellowship shall be 
final and no appeal would lie against any decision by NSFDC in regard 
thereof. 
viii. The results shall be publish ed on NSFDC website. Award letters can be 
downloaded from NSFDC online application portal or as advertised by 
NSFDC. 
9. Aadhar based I.D: 
Payment of Fellowships and any other admissible allowance should be made 
to beneficiaries through their bank accounts. Th e Ministry of Social Justice 
vide notification number 428 dated 16th February, 2017 has notified Aadhaar 
as an identity document under Section 7 of Aadhaar Act 2016 for all 
Scholarship Schemes w.e.f. 16th February 2017. NSFDC henceforth will make 
provisions for capturing details of Aadhaar, Aadhaar EID and other alternative 
documents as provided for in the said notification on their respective 
Scholarship portals or otherwise for disbursal of fellowship directly into the 
beneficiary's account. 
  
10. Disbursement of Fellowship 
Confirmation for continuation of the fellowship shall be given by the 
University twice a year on a semester basis within the 10th of the initial 
month of the semester. Based on this certification, the research scholars 
would be released the  fellowship within the 15th of every month without any 
further intervention of the university. The University will, however, be 
Page 7 of 7 
 
responsible for updating on the portal in case any research scholar 
discontinues the course, or is/has been on inadmissible leav e and thus not 
entitled for the fellowship during this period. 
In the event of discontinuation of the course or in case of inadmissible leave 
rendering him/her not entitled to the fellowship, the candidate will be 
responsible to intimate such position and he/she will be liable to refund the 
amount of over -payment, if any, of fellowship paid to him/her for the period 
beyond the date of such discontinuation, or for the such period of 
inadmissible leave. 
In case any research scholar absents without intimation  or is/ has been on 
admissible leave, the concerned Nodal Officer will be accountable to effect 
stopping of automatic generation of his/her fellowship and also to effect 
recovery of such over-payment, if any, made to the candidate. 
11. Evaluation and Monitoring  
i. A dashboard to monitor progress, geographic spread, discipline wise 
students enrolled, sanctions, completion of courses, etc. shall be put in 
place by NSFDC on its portal to be accessed by Ministry of Social Justice 
& Empowerment via login credential. NSFDC may provide the month-wise 
information (fresh+renewal) in terms of amount released and number of 
candidates to whom amount has been disbursed for fellowship to DoSJE 
regularly.    
ii. A grievance redressal portal and a call centre shall be set up by NSFDC  to 
take care of complaints of students availing of JRF/SRF by NSFDC. 
iii. Portal for online verification by the University for the students of NFSC 
will be developed by NSFDC. 
iv. Online portal for capturing the academic progress of scholars at different 
stages of fellowship will be developed by NSFDC. 
v. Implementation charges payable to NSFDC will be based on actual 
expenditure, subject to ceiling of 1% of the total fellowship disbursed by 
the NSFDC.

analysis.overview: The National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC) is a Government of India undertaking under the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, established on February 8, 1989, to promote socio-economic development of Scheduled Castes through financial assistance at concessional interest rates. It provides loans and grants via State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs) and other channel partners for income-generating activities, education, skill development, and fellowships. NSFDC implements various credit and non-credit schemes including Term Loan, Micro Finance Scheme, Educational Loan Scheme, Udyam Nidhi Yojana, Aajeevika Microfinance Yojana, and the National Fellowship for Scheduled Caste Students (NFSC).
objectives: Finance income generating schemes of eligible Scheduled Caste persons through State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs) and other Channelizing Agencies (CAs)
Improve flow of financial assistance to persons belonging to Scheduled Castes for their economic well-being
Provide Educational Loans to target group through SCAs and/or CAs for pursuing full-time professional/technical education in India and abroad
Provide grants for Skill Development Training Programmes of the target group
Organize/participate in Exhibitions cum Fairs for marketing of beneficiaries' products
Promote prosperity among Scheduled Castes through skill development and innovative initiatives
Identify trades and economic activities important to Scheduled Castes population
Upgrade skills and processes used by persons belonging to Scheduled Castes
analysis.eligibility: The beneficiary should be from the Scheduled Caste Community. Individuals, Partnership Firms and Co-operative Societies are eligible to undertake income generating activities. However, proposals submitted by Partnership Firms and Co-operative Societies shall be considered subject to the following: (a) All the members of Partnership Firms/Co-operative Societies belong to Scheduled Caste community (b) Annual family income of each member/applicant should not exceed Rs.5.00 lakh. Annual family income of the applicants should be within Rs.5.00 lakhs (for both rural and urban areas w.e.f. 07th January' 2026) under Credit Based Schemes. There is no income criterion under Skill Development Training Programmes. The verification of eligibility criteria shall be the sole responsibility of the SCAs/CAs. NSFDC shall, however, be entitled to re-verify the eligibility of the applicants, if it so desires.
analysis.benefits: Financial assistance at concessional interest rates under various credit schemes; loans up to 90% of project cost with varying maximum limits per scheme; interest rates charged to SCAs/CAs ranging from 2.5% to 5%, which in turn charge beneficiaries from 6.5% to 15%; repayment periods ranging from 3 to 12 years; moratorium periods from 3 months to 1 year; for non-credit schemes: 100% grant to Training Institute and stipend of Rs.1500 per month per trainee subject to 80%+ attendance; under NFSC: monthly fellowship of Rs.31,000 (initial 2 years) and Rs.35,000 (remaining tenure), contingency grants, HRA @8%-24%, escort/reader assistance of Rs.2,000/p.m. for disabled candidates.
analysis.financial_support: NSFDC provides loans up to 90% of the project cost with scheme-specific maximum limits: Term Loan (>Rs.1.25 lakh & upto Rs.45.00 lakh), Micro Finance Scheme (upto Rs.1.25 lakhs), Educational Loan Scheme (upto Rs.40.00 Lakh or 90% of course fee), Udyam Nidhi Yojana (upto Rs.4.50 lakhs), Aajeevika Microfinance Yojana (upto Rs.1.25 lakhs). Interest charged to SCAs/CAs ranges from 2.5% to 5% p.a., which is passed on to beneficiaries at 6.5% to 15% p.a. Repayment is in quarterly instalments with moratorium periods. For NFSC, fellowship is disbursed monthly via bank account after Aadhaar seeding and verification.
analysis.application_process: 1. Eligible target group (Scheduled Castes persons with annual family income up to Rs.5.00 lakhs) submits loan application to District Offices of State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs). 2. District Offices of SCAs/CAs scrutinize applications and forward them to their Head Offices. 3. SCAs/CAs appraise project viability and forward viable projects to NSFDC with recommendations. 4. NSFDC's Project Desk appraises proposals and submits report to Project Clearance Committee (PCC) for concurrence. 5. PCC-recommended proposals are sanctioned. 6. Sanction Letters (Letter of Intent - LOI) with Terms & Conditions are issued to SCAs/CAs for acceptance. 7. After acceptance of terms and fulfillment of Prudential Norms, funds are disbursed to SCAs/CAs on receipt of demand. 8. SCAs/CAs disburse funds to beneficiaries. 9. Beneficiaries repay loans as per stipulated repayment schedule.
required_documents: PIA letter + Artisan Card (for Cluster beneficiaries)
Partner Agency letter + SHG name (for SEED beneficiaries)
analysis.implementing_agency: National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC)
analysis.contact_details: Toll Free No.: 1800110396 | E-mail: nfsc.nsfdc@gmail.com | Email: cgm1nsfdc@gmail.com | Email: hr.nsfdc@gmail.com | Email: nsfdcskill@gmail.com | Email: rti.nsfdc@gmail.com | Email: nsfdcvigilance@gmail.com | Email: cdn.nsfdc@gmail.com | Email: cs.nsfdc@gmail.com | Email: support-nsfdc@nic.in
caveats: Direct contact or correspondence by Applicants/Beneficiaries with NSFDC shall not be entertained under any circumstances
Loans are to be repaid by beneficiaries as per repayment schedule stipulated by SCAs/CAs
For NFSC: Employees of any University/College/Educational Institution/Central/State/UT Government are excluded from availing Fellowship, even if on Study Leave or Extra Ordinary Leave
For NFSC: A scholar is eligible to receive only one fellowship at a time and must declare not to accept any other monetary benefit/scholarship/fellowship from any other source
For NFSC: A scholar who does not complete research work during fellowship period shall not be eligible to apply again under this scheme
For NFSC: Fellowship shall be granted only up to date of submission of Ph.D. thesis or five years of tenure, whichever is earlier; no extension beyond five years
For NFSC: Maximum allowed gap period from M.Phil. to Ph.D. is 2 years; no fellowship paid during gap period
For NFSC: Candidates who have secured admission must join fellowship within 3 months of JRF award letter issue; candidature cancelled if not joined within 3 months
source_cited_notes: https://nsfdc.nic.in/UploadedFiles/other/2025-12-30/standard operating procedure for selection of beneficiaries and conduct of exhibitions.pdf
